reviews.text: On 1/3/23 I called them and they scheduled me for a phone appointment on 1/6/23 at 2:30pm. At 2:50 pm I called and was advised that she (Katie Freeman) was just finishing up with a client and would call as soon as she could. At 3:50 pm after not hearing anything I called and was advised she would call within the hour. At 4:50 pm after again not hearing anything I called and -I- was asked if -I- wanted to reschedule. After saying no, I was told I was still on the calendar and would definitely be called that day. Never heard from them again. Take your business elsewhere they're too busy for new clients. Thank you for wasting my time.
reviews.ownerResponse: We're so sorry, Andrew.  We hope you were able to find someone to take your case.

reviews.text: The absolute worst experience at a time in my life that I needed honesty and stability. My marriage of 20 years ended. I hired Katie Freeman as my divorce attorney. It should have been a simple divorce. She was amazing at first then all communication stopped. I begged and pleaded many many many times for communication. My calls, texts, and emails would be avoided for weeks and a month at a time with no communication. My soon to be ex husbands lawyer finally had to have papers sent to her certified just to get contact. My divorce was final at the end of September. I have tried to contact her and Kayla her paralegal so many times to see if the pension paperwork was complete because she was still working on it. I STILL can not get a response. Thankfully I have saved all texts, emails, and voicemails as proof. I still have no idea about his pension, my child support, or my statement.
reviews.ownerResponse: We're sorry you are not happy with your experience, Emily.   Unfortunately, splitting up pension plans do require additional services not included with an underlying divorce case. We hope you were able to get these additional services from another attorney.

reviews.text: I was going through one of the most difficult situations in my life when I hired Katie. She was so kind and showed how much she cares for her clients. It’s not just a job to her,. It’s about helping families like mine fight for what is right. She went above and beyond to help me fight for my children’s wellbeing. She is extremely professional and help me every step of the way. I could never show say thank you enough for what she has done for my family. God bless you Katie!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Lynise.  We appreciate your kind words.  Katie always does a great job fighting for her clients.

reviews.text: Katie is an awesome attorney. I would recommend anyone to her. She was with me the whole time with my case and is very knowledgeable and professional. If you have any questions she will get you the answer. Her and her team are very trustworthy.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for the review, John!  We're so glad we were able to help!

reviews.text: Katie Freeman is the best! Honest and if she tells you something she will stick to it! In my divorce, I had to continually text and call her all times of the day. Wasn’t expecting her to answer until next business day but she did! Knows the law and has no problem standing up for your rights! Her knowledge and courtroom experience is hard to beat. If your in need of a attorney especially for family law, you must give her your first chance! You won’t regret it 😊
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Conrad!  We appreciate the kind review!  So glad we were able to help you!

reviews.text:
Not great at communicating during the case. I went a full month with no response despite multiple calls and emails. I eventually had to contact the main office to get someone to reply, instead of being able to reach my direct lawyer with the contact info I was given.

There is also a lack of transparency regarding financial status. I did not receive several invoices during my case, so I had no idea my funds were already depleted. When I was asked to pay at the end of the case, I was hit with a surprise bill. The office blames their invoicing system, but it felt like it was done purposely to gain more money.

Makenzie Arnold was absolutely on it from our very first call. Before I even paid a retainer, she jumped on a consultation call, reached out to the opposing attorney to gather more information, and came back to me with a clear picture of what she could do and what it would cost. That kind of initiative right out of the gate told me everything I needed to know.

I hired her, and she and her team were incredibly efficient throughout the entire process. Everything produced was accurate, thorough, and strong.

Makenzie is sharp, quick, and genuinely cares about her clients.  I'd recommend her without hesitation.

Morgan Woelke, was incredibly kind, professional, and supportive, patiently guiding me through every step of the process and always making sure I felt informed, confident, and well assisted throughout my case.

Makenzie Arnold is the real deal.
My first attorney left family law 6 months into our complicated custody case. That was scary at first, but turned out to be a gift, because then I hired Makenzie Arnold. Immediately, I knew I was in more capable hands.

I've spent nearly 3 years now with Makenzie by my side and can confirm that every positive review about this woman is 100% accurate.
I wholeheartedly recommend her. She is one of the most authentic human beings I know and easily one of the most competent, badass attorneys in NWA.

Makenzie carries herself with integrity.
She balances compassion and kindness with no-nonsense honesty.
She's well-educated and has a sharp, strategic mind and great sense of humor. She has a good working relationship with her colleagues and is respected by local judges.

Makenzie has excellent communication skills. She consistently responded to my emails in a timely manner, and the vast majority of the time, I dealt directly with her.
She never made me feel like my questions were silly or my concerns unfounded. She took the time to explain what was happening and what it meant for us, every step of the way.

Makenzie is steadfast in her pursuit of justice. She is patient and prepared. Calm and composed. She stayed steady every time I broke down.
She is fierce in the courtroom and the way she fights for her clients makes it evident she truly cares. I never wondered if our family mattered to her. I knew we weren't just a job and I was confident she was giving us her very best the entire time.
The legal field needs more like Makenzie Arnold.

In addition to years of intensive therapy, Makenzie has played an instrumental role in my healing journey as an abuse survivor. She helped me feel brave and powerful at a time when I felt very afraid.
I'm so thankful I trusted her to guide us to a favorable outcome. She will have my respect and gratitude forever for fighting to keep my child safe.
Johnathan is an amazing lawyer. I was new to all this. He was patient and helped me through my situation. I didn’t miss a day of work and he represented me through the whole case.

They don't answer their own phone, outsourcing it to people who can't even tell me if you can handle an S Corp move to Arkansas. "They do a lot of things, like DUIs and divorces" isn't an answer and is irrelevant to my call.

From the very first conversation I had with Carter, I felt supported and confident about my case. He immediately put me at ease with his professionalism and kindness, and I knew I was in capable hands. What stood out most was his responsiveness—every single time I reached out, he got back to me right away without delay. That level of attentiveness made me feel truly cared for throughout the entire process. Carter’s expertise, steady guidance, and dedication ultimately delivered the outcome we had hoped for, and I couldn’t be more grateful. I highly recommend him to anyone in need of an attorney who is not only skilled, but also compassionate and dependable.

If you don't want to wake up every morning thinking about how Ben Catterlin stole $10,000 of your hard earned money, then I would not hire him. Lying to your face about how he would defend you with a strategic defense, but then when you are all paid he changes his tune to align with the prosecution. The prosecutor, whose sole job is to get to the truth, not just prosecute, couldn't even look at me, she was just out to get me not the truth.
The judge, well, that is just another joke. There is no justice folks and there are no sharks in Washington/Benton County's district. This guy told us he was going for a stand your ground defense until 5 days before.the trial, then he laughs and says derisively, we'll it was just a little dog, if you had hit a bigger dog in the pack of dogs attacking you... Of course this was after he took the $10,000. He took both my husband & my case and he didn't even do one thing for my husband. Not even his own consult before the trial.  We will be reporting this to the bar association.
If this review stops one dollar from going to this pitiful injustice driving machine. Amen.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. Maltese,
Im sorry you feel that way about the outcome of your case. Unfortunately, when you stab a 16 year old small, blind dog- place the dog into a sealed container to die and then stab it with a pitchfork repeatedly- the State of Arkansas is going to want you to go to prison. You approached me with a "stand your ground" defense, however that was not available to you given the circumstances of your case. The fact that you are now a free person and not in jail or prison, should tell you how great of an outcome you received in your case. Please enjoy the free air as a daily reminder of the outcome I was able to achieve on your case.

reviews.text: Morgan Woelke helped us with our immigration process. She was great to work with - professional, experienced, and thorough with her work. She went above and beyond to make sure everything flowed smoothly. We recommend working with her and would work with her again.

I want to thank my lawyer Jonathan Faught for taking charge of my case. Excellent service. If I could give 10 stars I would. I had a house for theft of lease property, no driver's license and no insurance, and my lawyer took care of everything. In less than 6 months, my case was resolved. Thank you Jonathan Faught for helping me. Excellent service.

I would not recommend this law firm to anyone at first everything seemed professional and good however. I had a payment agreement set in place and I had to call 3 different times for the fact that they did not want to honor the agreement and tried to charge me on when I wasn’t supposed to be charged and I had to call three different times to get that fixed. not only was there an issue with billing My court date was canceled for the fact that all of the attorneys and judge agreed on everything I wasn’t even able to explain myself I never agreed to anything and then when I emailed my lawyer, I got an email from a different person at the office saying that I’m terminated so they refuse to talk to me. This law firm has very questionable ethics and I would not recommend to anybody

I am updating my review due to serious concerns about Ben's performance on my case. We hired Ben over a year ago on a felony case and paid $5000.00. I've noticed a significant decline in his responsiveness, and it’s concerning especially as trial approaches next month. He has not followed up with the witnesses we provided, and hasn’t told us how he plans to defend in court. Several calls and emails have gone unanswered, leaving us in a troubling situation.

So far, pending waiting to get more information. Will update after trial as well.
reviews.ownerResponse: Adri, we apologize for any communication issues but the reason you have not heard anything is because there is nothing to share. This is very common, we are most likely waiting on the state to give us the information we need. We would have preferred you set up an in person meeting if this was bothering you instead of making a review with little to no information on what’s going on with the process. Please feel free to call and set up that meeting and I’m sure we can figure this out.

reviews.text:
The first time I used Ben Catterlin was a few years back and he was expensive but also helpful.  I did have to schedule an in office visit to get our questions answered because he wasn't very informative via emails. This was the first time I ever retained a lawyer so I assumed all lawyers acted the same way and were almost impossible to make contact with.
Recently I referred him to 2 different friends of mine.  His performance was inexcusable to say the least.  The first friend is now in prison.  I don't feel he did his best to defend her.  He never met with her in person.  2 days before her trial he called her on the phone.  During the trial he never presented the defense she requested him to use.  She paid him $5000 and he only spoke to her twice before the trial.
The other friend I referred him to had even more disappointment with his services.  He never returned any of the clients phone calls.
He did reply to a few emails.  He filed papers to the court saying he would represent the client, and he made one court appearance to enter a plea and request a jury trial.  He was paid $7500 in Nov 2024 for representation.  The client passed away in December 2024.  The case was then dismissed.  However there was still the case to return the clients property that was confiscated during the arrest, which had a value of well over $40,000.   The clients heirs/estate administrator (the clients daughter) tried to discuss the case with Ben Catterlin but did not receive any satisfaction.  He never returned any of her phone calls. so she hired another attorney. The new attorney discovered documents she was never made aware of such as counter offers from the prosecuting attorney and all the property confiscated during the initial arrest was going to be auctioned in 2 days.  Luckily the new attorney has been very helpful.  When she told Ben Catterlin that she no longer wanted his representation he became very hostile.  She requested a refund and he refused.  Saying he didn't have to give her a dime.  However, she had a previous email from him sent shortly after her father passed away saying he would refund the $7500.  After telling him that he has agreed to refund $2500.  I would not use Ben Catterlin for anything.

After my experience, I have to say this is the best law firm out there! Ben was professional, approachable, and available for any question that I had. He had my best interest in mind all while keeping my spirits up!  I would recommend Ben Catterlin to anyone in need of representation.

I cannot thank Payton Hamric enough for the incredible support and expertise she provided during my divorce and custody case. Payton's kindness, understanding, and responsiveness truly set her apart as a top-notch lawyer. Throughout the entire process, she eased my fears and made me feel like I had a dedicated advocate fighting for me and my children.

Payton went above and beyond to ensure that every detail was taken care of and that our best interests were always the priority. She brought up important considerations that I hadn't even thought of, showing me just how dedicated she was to securing the best possible outcome for us.

Thanks to Payton's hard work and dedication, my kids and I are now in a much better situation post-divorce. I am forever grateful for the positive difference she has made in our lives. If you are looking for a compassionate, knowledgeable, and dedicated lawyer, look no further than Payton Hamric. She is truly a gem in the legal field and I will never forget all that she has done for us!

Makenzie Arnold at Catterlin & Arnold Law Firm was an absolute lifesaver during one of the hardest times in my life. I was in an abusive relationship, scared, and unsure of how to move forward. From the very first consultation, she was different from the other lawyers I spoke with—she didn’t just listen, she *fought* for me. When I was too afraid to think clearly, she was my strength.

My ex-husband tried to keep me off financial documents, but Makenzie wouldn’t let that slide. She made sure I got what was *fair*, not just what was easy. She also helped me stay on top of local law enforcement, who too often don’t take the time to truly protect women in these situations. Her dedication and persistence made all the difference.

I would recommend Makenzie to *anyone* needing a fierce yet compassionate advocate. She has a heart of gold and the tenacity to match. If you're looking for an attorney who will truly stand up for you, look no further. 💛

Had a pleasure working with Morgan at this firm. Job well done. Any immigration issues or filing I have to do I would go with them. Great team at the firm. Easy to get a hold of, easy to talk to, and Very Knowledgeable about everything needed with applying for my P.R , Travel Visa, and work authorization card. TOP TIER Service!

From the onset of my immigration status adjustment process, I was fortunate to have the guidance of Ms. Morgan Woelke, an outstanding immigration lawyer from the Catterlin & Arnold Law Firm. Her expertise was evident in every interaction. She meticulously reviewed my case, ensuring all documentation was accurate and submitted promptly.

Her proactive approach and clear communication were instrumental in navigating the complex immigration landscape. She was always available to answer my questions and provided regular updates, which greatly alleviated my stress during this critical time.

Morgan's thorough understanding of immigration law and policy nuances allowed her to anticipate potential issues and address them proactively.

Thanks to her dedication and proficiency, my immigration status adjustment was a success. I am deeply grateful for her role in this pivotal moment of my life and highly recommend Ms. Morgan Woelke to anyone seeking competent and compassionate immigration legal services.
Great response time and advice.

I was recently represented by Mr. Jonathan Faught. I won’t disclose case details but suffice it to say Mr. Faught credited me with exceptional representation and communication skills. All emails were answered within hours and not days like with a few attorneys, and he was very punctual arriving to court on my behalf. I really have nothing but great things to say about him and this law firm. Highly recommended!

Rebecca Bryson is amazing! I felt represented and respected from the very first conversation we had until the custody/paternity case was over. In the end, I got the what I was asking for thanks to her and her firm.

6/5 ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Highly recommend

If I could give 0 stars I would! I got my divorce in 2021 and if Ms. Arnold did not drag her feet I would have been divorced a lot soon, but she refused to listen to me. Along with other complaints I was worried about. I recommend anybody else but her.

Refused to prepare for trial, assisted the prosecution to convict his client. Violated rule 33.1 trying to waive his clients case that there was no evidence against him, by violating this rule it waived the issue on appeal. He helped the prosecution by deliberately making false statements in his opening argument. Establishing probable cause when there was no probable cause. Many other violations of not requesting the judge to read the jury instructions on the elements of the crime. See law suit being filed in Federal court for details, it's going to be filed this week , details will follow on next phase.

I worked with Melanie Clatanoff on a high conflict divorce involving custody arrangements. I felt she was truly on my side and listened to what I wanted while still offering solid strategy on how to get to the finish line. It was a relief knowing I could trust her to advocate for me. She kept me informed and was available to answer questions I had along the way. Will absolutely work with Melanie again if I ever have other legal needs!
Melanie is wonderful! Highly recommended!! Caring and patient, understanding, and professional.
Scheduled a consultation regarding my son's "father's" rights and threats he's made recently. I'm very prepared and informed at the end of my consult.

My experience at the law firm with Rebecca   was not good. If I asked her to do any work on my case I was told if you expect me to do the work its going to cost you thousands of dollars.
Still cost me almost 4,000.  Had to hire a new attorney which took over the case from start to finish. Cost less then Rebecca & I didn't have to deal with her drama anymore  what a relief 😌  to get away from Rebecca.  I have know ideal why they had five star under my name seen it & had to fix it! My new attorney was amazing he did the work!

Speaking from my own personal experience! Ben was my attorney for my case. Him and his team were responsive, polite and professional through my entire case which lasted 10 months. He really eased my mind about the situation and answered any questions that I had, even after my case ended. He’s also very humble which is how your attorney should be with you! Every dollar spent on him was well worth it. Ben will be my attorney of choice should I ever need a lawyer in the future! :)
Top notch law firm! Very pleased with Rebecca and the outcome.
The office communicates effectively and works hard for their clients to get a favorable outcome. Miss Arnold was a good choice for my situation and much appreciated.
Friendly, respectful, willing to help any way possible.
Makenzie hands down is worth every penny and more! She was a beast with my case. Would recommend her all day everyday. She stayed on point the whole time💪
Initially offered good advice which saved me money. Represented me well with legal issues in court.

Absolute worst law firm I’ve ever dealt with and I have hired several in the past. If you have any kind of emergency issue, don’t expect them to return to you with an answer in a reasonable amount of time. You’ll end up getting screwed because it’ll take Melanie a 5 days to respond. I’ve waited for almost a month for Melanie to return an email. I followed up with the receptionist who is the most flippant person I’ve ever dealt with and incredibly unhelpful. When I went to go pay my retainer fee, I walked in and was greeted by someone who looked like they just walked out of a yoga class. Very unprofessional. It’s been almost 2 weeks and I’ve heard nothing from my follow up phone call regarding the original email I sent. Sure doesn’t take Melanie any time to respond to other attorneys in a chain email but if you’re the client, she obviously doesn’t care.

Rebecca is amazing!!! I will never be able to thank her enough for everything she has done and continues to do for me and my sweet little boy! She helped us quickly with emergency custody, a PO, and a divorce that included quite a bit. Rebecca fights hard but is also very empathic and supportive! I have never once felt alone in this season of my life because of her! Rebecca is a blessing and I’m so thankful for her! She truly cares about her clients, I never felt like I was just another case to her. If your reading this review and going through a divorce, child custody, or needing to be protected with a PO reach out to her!

Well, it seemed to start out good. But eventually it got confusing. I paid WELL OVER $1k to start my case and thought "okay finally I am going to get some help". A little paperwork was filed and we went to court for 11 minutes once with a rescheduling for February this year. While waiting for the court date/court I started to notice things were being ignored by my lawyer and things were being left out of discovery that other lawyers were saying needed to be there. The judge got mad at me for things this woman lawyer did not turn over in time when i had to got ot court alone. Unanswered emails & phone calls & questions about my case were also ignored. I was spoken to pretty rudely by my lawyer in emails telling me she is not a charity and such. My lawyer did not even tell me the court date for jury trial in February of this year, I had to find that out on my own. My lawyer witnessed the man following/stalking me and she did not once even help me get any paperwork to keep him at bay. She now tells me I owe over her $1k again for past work on top of the WELL OVER 1K I ALREADY PAID and when I tried to offer to pay her in March of this year she dropped my case and sent a letter to the court saying she dropped my case within moments. I feel I was taken advantage of and abandoned right in the middle of a case. I thought I could trust her. I think my lawyer was to emotional. I wish this was not the case. Best of luck to anyone who hires them but I would try for another lawyer elsewhere.. God bless

If you're here, reading the reviews (to try and make a decision on whether or not you want to hire Austin) then you are doing exactly what I did. I searched around and got 'ehh' vibes on many lawyers. After reading Austin's reviews, it just felt right. I had a good feeling about what others were saying about him. So, I took a chance and hired him. I have no regrets about doing so. He is super easy to work with. You can easily talk to him like a friend or relative and he never made me feel like I was less than knowledgeable.

I realize that a good lawyer is more than just talking a good talk, to appease their clients. They need to know the law. Once again, I have no regrets on his skills as an attorney.  He was sharp and very informative. I've had very poor relations with attorneys (over the years) who took my money and either split with no representation or brought mediocre skills to the table. Austin, you made my situation a lot more easy than what I expected. Thank you, brother.

So, if you're still searching and feel a bit overwhelmed by your situation, take a chance on this man. He did me right. I realize how good reviews can give light to someone's character and skills. That is why I'm here writing this. To possibly help the next person make a decision.

If you're ready to end your search and move forward... stop here and give the man a call. And good luck with your situation. 🙂
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your kind words, Brian! I enjoyed getting to know you and helping you out!

reviews.text:
Good. Fully recommended.

Austin Lucas, as an attorney, is noble, moral, dedicated, and deliberately effective. One can assert that he attempts to carry over his personal values well into his profession, and the client is almost surely guaranteed to benefit from that. His responses and resolutions are timely, compassionate, considerate and never hasty.
Albeit colloquially an attorney his like may be called a "shark," I suppose it suffices to say that he's exceptionally goal oriented and dignified in his approach. In that, he is the falcon.
Will not hesitate to hire Mr. Lucas in the future, nor would I hesitate to recommend to friends or family.

By no means is he a pushover attorney, and he will play his cards professionally without backing down! We payed thousands to another attorney who couldn't even do anything close to what Austin has done for my son and his future!

His character is not often seen in this crazy world and his ability to make you laugh even if you don't want to makes all the difference when things got rough.

reviews.text:
Where do I start? More than a year ago, I had something that was unexpected and traumatizing to happen to me. After assessing the damages and my injuries, I knew someone would need to be held accountable for what had happened. I didn't know where to start or who to call. I was hurt and a little bit afraid. Several hours after I was able to gather myself and nurse my injuries, I started searching things online. I came across a couple of places that sounded as if they may be able to give me direction. Needless to say Lavar Law Firm was the only place that even responded to me. The initial contact and process to get things started fairly easy and stress-free. I was given options as to how to complete and return necessary documents and paperwork. Throughout the course of my treatment, I did receive a few periodic checks from the staff regarding updates, progress, questions, and/or concerns for not just them but for me as well. The lines of communication were always open. Anytime I reached out to them, they always addressed me and responded in a timely manner. If I should ever have an issue such as this, would I use their services again? I'm more than likely would. If I had a family or friend that may need assistance with a similar issue would I recommend them? I most likely would. Were they professional at all times with me? They most definitely were. When I didn't know what to do regarding my case or treatment, or felt like I was running into a brick wall, did they offer me suggestions? They most certainly did. I do realize there is only so much that a law office that represents you can do. Even though I was advised of what would happen regardless of the direction I decided to go, I still feel like the party we settled with didn't look at the full scope of my injury. No I did not require surgery and they were focused on an MRI result. However, I still have pain. I'm still taking medication. And I may never heal. I have permanent scarring that I didn't have prior. It changed the way I feel about my parents. It changes the way that I look at myself. I'm no longer able to do things that I was able to do before. I'm not able to dress the way that I was able to before. I really do wish we could have fought a little harder. But I totally get it. Those who aren't doing the suffering don't tend to fight as hard.

Now this that I am about to say is not directed towards my case in any way. But this is just a little tidbit of advice and an opinion. When speaking to one of the representatives I asked a question regarding an unrelated issue that I felt like I needed legal advice on. I really wish that law offices had a community or circle of friends. I understand different attorneys specialize in different things. But there's never anything wrong with being able to refer your current clients to another reputable attorney that may be able to help them. I say that because I'm used to working in an environment where we had what we call Community partners. If we weren't able to help them, we would refer them to someone who could. And the only reason that I'm saying this is because I asked the question and I got referred to an agency that I had already reached out to look what I was asking was for a recommendation for legal advice.

But overall I am very happy and pleased with the business that I did or had at this point with Lavar law firm.

reviews.text:
Feeling the call to adopt? Look no further than Heimer Law.

Brandy, Brittney, Jess, and Justin are so much more than an agency.

Brandy was our AP advocate and walked us through the process step by step (more than once). The team at Heimer truly stands in your corner, and in the world of adoption, having a true advocate makes all the difference. They care deeply and personally for the families they serve.

Brittney, the birth parent advocate, was refreshingly honest. She offered thoughtful, compassionate advice to help us comfort and support our birth mother.

Jess went above and beyond—literally meeting us on a Sunday, after hours, to notarize our adoption so we could bring our little boy home. We couldn’t be more grateful.

And Justin—his reputation as a family attorney is second to none. From the very start, we knew we were in the best and most capable hands.

If I could give them 6 stars, I would. But even that wouldn’t capture the depth of gratitude my wife and I feel for this organization, its team, and its leadership. If you’re looking to complete your family, your search ends here.

reviews.text: It is so surprising seeing all of these 5 star reviews after the way my daughter was treated and serviced by Justin and this firm.  They did not act in her best interest knowing all she had been through with past situations!  They paid them to act in their best interest and it did not happen in no way or fashion.  Very unprofessional and unethical; which professional attorneys are to abide by.  They did not follow through in their responsibilities and left my daughter empty handed again!  I would never recommend this firm for adoption agency. However, I am very happy their have been some who has left happy and satisfied.  I would just have given anything that one of these would’ve been my daughter as well.  She has truly struggled for the past 3 years with loss after loss and they were very aware of the struggles.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. Roussel,
I am sorry for your grief. While, as I am sure you understand, I can't speak to the specifics of any client situation, I will express my sympathies that your daughter's adoption plan did not work out as she had hoped. As you stated, she had been on a long and painful road for years before coming to Heimer Law. Adoption can be a difficult journey. All or our clients are aware that there is a risk of the biological parents changing their mind and that the child is simply not theirs until the adoption has been completed. It is extremely painful for the hopeful adoptive parents when this happens, especially if they had similar painful experiences in the past. While I wholly disagree that we were unprofessional, unethical, or failed to follow through on our responsibilities, I understand your disappointment at the outcome of the matter. In the end, we cannot and would not guarantee that any adoption will be successful, it's simply not within our power to do so. Instead, we work to diligently and carefully provide for our clients and facilitate their adoption journey. I wish you and your family the best moving forward.
Regards,
Justin Heimer
Heimer Law

Justin and his staff were nothing short of amazing!! We had a very long, and complicated adoption. Not only were they very knowledgeable, but also ethical and compassionate. But most importantly they were very helpful, respectful, and considerate to our Birthmother. At one point our Birthmother was admitted into the hospital under emergent and unexpected circumstances. The social worker took time away from her family and sat with her the whole night while we were being precautions and being quarantined at home. We had a 7.5+ month  match with our Birthmother and then bio dad contested the adoption, prolonging the journey even further. But we are happy to say, that after the contested adoption, a four (4) month postponement by the judge, and a two (2) hour virtual hearing; that a long seven (7) months later Justin won our case for us. So we were able to add  our 1.5 year olds biological half sister to our family; forever and always.

We will always be grateful for Heimer Law.

Thank you;
The Martins

I was referred to Justin after two disrupted adoptions with my agency. The lawyers that I had worked with were a whole nightmare. Justin walked us through step by step what we needed to do in order to terminate the previous attorney's contract and transfer the files to his office.  We are SO GLAD we did! Our experience with Heimer Law felt like a team effort instead of the coercion and shadiness we experienced with our previous attorney.  Heimer Law even went above and beyond after our adoption was final to get our birth certificate corrected multiple times (we have tricky names which I guess the Arkansas Dept. of Health found hard to spell the first few tries). We recently completed our 3rd adoption with Heimer Law and they were by far the best attorney/law firm that we have worked with. The team as a whole was responsive and caring throughout our whole process, and all took time out of their busy schedules to answer all of our questions that came up and talk us through the process and the ways things could proceed differently in the ever changing Covid situation. When our son was born under different circumstances than any of us were prepared for they continued to be so kind and caring and walked us through all the changes and made sure everything worked out for both us and his first family. More than anything else they were by far the most ethical attorney/firm that we have worked with. They were able to answer all of our questions prior to our son being born, and as everything panned out we didn’t even need to question their actions because they again went above and beyond to make sure the law was followed. Desirae typically got the blunt of things when we were tired and frustrated, but always responded well, and ensured us that adoption is not always rainbows and cupcakes, but the end result is so worth it! We are so grateful for the work that they have done for our little family, and they were right, the end result is worth every ounce of ourselves that we put into the process!

COVID-19 put a wrench in some of our plans, but they made sure to go above and beyond to guarantee all of our questions were answered, even when sometimes the answer was “we just wait.”

reviews.text: If you are a father, don't use this guy. We had my husband's son for 3 years and then his biological mother decided that she wanted to be a part of his life. She took him for what was supposed to have been a 2 week visit and called 2 days later and told us that she was keeping him and she was taking us to court for money. We hired Ryan as our family attourney. Even with his biological mother uprooting him, she still got custody? My husband is a decorated veteran who served in Afghanistan over seas for a year and did not deserve to have his child taken from him. She got sole custody (he could have faught for joint) they also factored in his alleged "built in overtime" into his child support payments. I also pushed very hard to get his son treatment for his ADHD for several years. 2 weeks into the school year it took a teacher telling her that he needs some attention in order for the biological mother to consider it as a factor. That is the worst part is seeing this child uprooted from his home into an unknown environment without his family or his brothers. My husband is a good man and good man and he deserved someone who would have fought a little harder. The regression when we did see him was substantial. We potty trained him 3 times and taught him his ABC's and ro count to 100 three times. No one really looked at what the child needed or sat down to speak with the child. All they wanted to talk about was income and overtime.

I wanted to give an updated review and honestly I should’ve done it sooner. I was able to finally get divorced and really, the delays made it worth it because the irony of having my divorce finalized on Valentine’s Day is delicious.
Don't know

This is the first time I've ever needed to hire a lawyer for personal injury sustained at work.  I checked around and Jason was referred to me by a couple contacts.   I looked at several websites and decided to give Jason a call.   I was very anxious and was in a good amount of pain at the time.   Jason took way more time with me on the phone than I expected him to.   From Pre surgery all the way through to the end of my case, Jason and his hard working team exceeded my expectations!   The attention to detail, personal service, communications, response time, and overall truly caring about my case has destroyed any pre conceived notions or stereotypes I ever had about lawyers or needing one.   Jason is not greedy and charges a very fair fee compared to all of the other Personal Injury Attorneys in the Northwest Arkansas Area that I checked out before choosing Jason Hatfield.   All I can say is that it was the best decision I could have made, he won my case without even having to go to court, was able to get the other party to pay the attorney fees, and was a pleasure to have on my side.  Thank you for putting up with me Jason, lol.   I was a nervous wreck and you were able to calm me down and explain everything so that it made sense.

Sincerely and truly grateful for your service,

Matthew Taylor

reviews.text: I went to Greg because he said that he was a constitutional lawyer. He got felony charges against me dropped, but failed to get a resisting arrest charge dropped as well, although the resisting arrest charge was apart of the same incident as the other felony charges. The Springdale police decided to throw the book at me after I stood up to them and explained to them that I had a right to carry a firearm. I was open carrying a firearm and the police were checking me out, although we’re living in a constitutional carry state, the law enforcement community are seeking to target Arkansans who carry around firearms. He was to afraid to rock the boat and stand up against law enforcement. If he would have persisted the video evidence as we discussed then the jury would have seen the police officer when they stated their real reason for stoping me. They stoped me because some concerned citizen was calling the police that someone was carrying around a firearm in their neighborhood. That’s right, I was carrying around a firearm, holstered and on my hip. I really would like to teach law enforcement a lesson, especially when it comes to my constitutional rights in this country. Just because someone is walking around carrying a firearm doesn’t give law enforcement a green light to verify their identity.
reviews.ownerResponse: I got this man’s felony terroristic threatening and battery charge dismissed, despite a violent altercation with police that left an officer needing surgery and despite his telling the cops that he had a right to kill them!  He was later charged with misdemeanor resisting arrest based on the same incident. Though convicted in Springdale City Court and sentenced to jail in the second case, I managed to get the prosecutor to waive the sentence provided we didn’t appeal it to a jury trial. But my client insisted on taking it to a jury nonetheless and got slammed. While I am neither a perfect lawyer nor a perfect man, it’s beyond me how I could have handled these matters any better than I did.

I recently scheduled an appointment with David Lee at Miller Butler and had a very disappointing experience. While I understand that being sick is unavoidable, the way the situation was handled was extremely unprofessional. On the morning of my appointment, I received a last-minute text canceling our meeting, despite having taken time off work to attend.

David then proceeded to text me, claiming he had attempted to call me prior to the appointment, which was untrue. All communication had been via text, and no effort was made to collect my information or offer alternate ways for me to provide details about my case, such as an email address. Despite my efforts to share as much information as possible with his assistant beforehand, it was clear that no preparation had been done.

I understand that being sick is unplanned, but what I find unprofessional is the overall lack of organization, communication, and preparation. To add to this, David expressed repeated concerns via text about not accepting my MetLife insurance due to prior negative experiences with them. This was emphasized multiple times, making me feel that my case was not being treated with care or professionalism.

While this was my experience, I recommend others use caution when working with this law firm.

If you are a father fighting for your kids, I’m telling you right now run to Victoria Bruton as fast as possible.

After a decade of navigating the court system amid a high-conflict custody case, I've faced numerous challenges: false accusations, lies, and slander. While engaging with the most prominent attorneys in NWA, I never felt heard. They simply wanted to wrap up the case as easily as possible which meant they expected me to give into ridiculous demands. When the opposing party starts out at ridiculous and you start out at normal; the compromise usually ends up being halfway between completely insane and normal. Victoria is different. She gets it. She understands how important fathers are in a child’s life. She isn’t afraid of a good fight. She exhibits unparalleled attention to detail, professionalism and determination. Unlike others who prioritized ease, Victoria fiercely advocated for me, uncovering critical details such as my ex's affair with a married man. She fought for me like a game bred pit bull.

I’m not exaggerating you’re getting the Nick Saban of family attorneys when you hire Victoria. My rights were suspended due to a false accusation in an ex parte hearing. Victoria went in there and it wasn’t even close. It was like that time Georgia played Florida State in 2023. She left the opposing attorney struggling to put together coherent sentences. She caught my ex in numerous lies on the stand and skewered their so called professional witness. I almost felt bad for them…almost

I’m not exaggerating when I say if my life was on the line I would hire Victoria. The only reason I’m leaving this review is bc I’ve been in your shoes. I’ve felt the hopelessness you’re experiencing. I never want anyone to feel like that. Victoria literally saved my family and my world.

If you’re reading this review bc you want to learn about Victoria bc your ex hired her, get ready. It’s gonna be a long day.

David Lee worked our case and did an exceptional job. He is professional, polite, helpful and happy to work with you on your case. He communicates well through out the process, he is patient with any questions or concerns you may have and explains everything thoroughly so you know exactly what’s going on.

I regret to say that my experience with this law firm, particularly attorney Kate Hill, was incredibly disappointing. I scheduled a consultation appointment with Kate after her assistant assured me that there were no conflicts of interest that would prevent her from representing me. However, after Kate spoke with my ex-wife's attorney, who happened to be my opposition, she suddenly claimed that a conflict existed, rendering her unable to represent me in any future matters.

It is deeply concerning that Kate's decision seemed to be influenced by conversations with the opposing party's attorney. This indicates a lack of integrity and independence on her part, which are essential qualities for any lawyer. A lawyer's primary duty should be to advocate for their client's best interests, not to bow to pressure from the other side.

Overall, my experience with this law firm, particularly with attorney Kate Hill, has left me questioning their professionalism and commitment to their clients. I would caution anyone considering their services to proceed with extreme caution, as their actions suggest a weak and easily swayed legal representation.

Victoria has represented me in 4 cases at 2 different law firms for civil and criminal. She's great, cares about her clients and highly recommended. She is a bulldog in the courtroom and won everything she represented me in. Thank you!

My wife and I were very satisfied with Matt Fryar's services. Setting up our will and accompanying documents was quick and painless, and Matt made sure that we understand the process and the associated costs. Not sure anymore why we put this off for so long.

My husband and I had a trust done in 2001, then "life happened." We no longer had the assets to require a trust, so had a few simple wills done in the interim. 25 years later, our family and financial situations have changed enough that we wanted to be very specific regarding our future planning as well as having our end-of-life documents updated. Having been a legal secretary for many years, I know where to look at credentials in addition to reading public reviews. I began researching NWA law firms, narrowed my preferred options down to three, and used their web forms to request information. One ignored my specific information requests and one didn't reply at all. The third, who responded first and completely answered my questions, was the obvious choice, and we couldn't have been more pleased with our experience.

We were immediately comfortable with Matt Fryar when we met him for our initial consultation at the end of October. Matt is experienced and professional as well as friendly and very easy to talk to. He explained everything clearly and easily answered all of our questions. The fee for the work we required was very reasonable, and we made the decision to have him prepare our trust at that meeting.

Matt collected all of the necessary information that we could provide before we left his office and sent us on our way with a list of "homework" including specific bequests, confirmation of executors and decision-makers, etc., and asked us when we expected to be ready to complete the documents. Because it was close to the holidays, we chose a date in early January, but a family emergency requiring us to travel caused us to postpone completion until February.

I have experience in completing and witnessing will and trust documents both as a client and as a law firm employee, and this was the best experience I have ever had!  Matt's comfortable style, friendly demeanor and humor made the session pleasant and entertaining. The session was streamlined and simple, with everything clearly stated and explained as Matt went through each page with us, and the two witnesses were also friendly.

We enthusiastically recommend Matt Fryar, and by extension, his firm for your legal needs. We won't look anywhere else for our own!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for this review -- it means a lot to us that we met your expectations! It was an absolute privilege to work with you.

reviews.text: My wife and I contacted Matt Fryar to help us with our estate planning.  Matt was very professional, yet we immediately felt at ease discussing our finances. He asked detailed questions concerning our wishes. His fees were extremely reasonable and his knowledge with trusts is impressive. Thank you Matt.

Garrett may be from Blytheville, but doggone it, he's a good attorney. Hiring Garrett  was the best decision I made ! This guy is a legal wizard who turned my messy situation into a win faster than you can say “objection!” From the moment I met him, Garrett was all in—accessible, straightforward, and with a no-nonsense vibe that made me trust him instantly. He handled my case with the precision of a chef perfecting a soufflé, keeping me in the loop like I was his VIP client (which, let’s be honest, I totally was!).
His honesty is refreshing—none of that lawyer mumbo-jumbo where you’re left guessing. He laid out my options with a grin, like we were plotting a heist, and delivered results that had me doing a happy dance. Whether it’s criminal defense or family law, Garrett’s got the skills and the heart—plus, I bet he’d win a courtroom comedy contest with those quick wits! If you need an attorney who’s part superhero, part stand-up comedian, call Garrett Harlan. Five stars !

reviews.text: I hired Garrett as a lawyer for a criminal case in which I was falsely accused of felonies. After the first initial conversation that we had in his office he pretty much blew me off after that. I had him file for a freedom information act with the court and which we waited 4 months to get any kind of information back(Way too long). Some of the evidence and dash cams was not even turned into the court. When I ask about said dash cams and evidence he tells me that he did not know that he could ask for anything else under the freedom of information act. He also never went over any evidence, videos, police reports, or anything.We talked about nothing. Two business days before my court date he calls me and tells me that if I do not plead guilty then I will go do 12 years in the Arkansas department of corrections. When I ask to see any of the evidence he got very upset and told me that he is the one that filed for the information act not me and it is none of my business what the court has gave him. Still to this day one year later, almost,I have seen nothing from his office, but the police report which was falsified. The only reason why he gave me this is because I had to do a anger management assessment and I had to have the police report to get into the facility. I actually had to go the prosecuting attorney in Benton county to file myself for a freedom of information act to get any documents or videos. He did not tell the court that the police held my mother and myself for 4 hours outside in 110° heat while they violated our civil rights,by not giving myself an attorney they also illegally and unwillingly went into my house stole a gun took pictures of a gun and then put the gun back. The state trooper falsified documents went back and made the victim changed their story so that they did not get charges on them. I feel Garrett did not do what a attorney should do for their client in court especially when he tells his client that not only the charges was upscaled but the police violated my civil rights. He has an obligation with his client to tell the court before I plead guilty to any charges about the violations and he failed to act accordingly as a lawyer under the oath of the Constitution that he said he would. I feel that he just did not care about myself as a client and I would not recommend him or this law firm to anybody for any reason!!!
reviews.ownerResponse:
We take our responsibility to every client seriously and are committed to providing ethical, thorough, and respectful legal representation. We never force or pressure any client to plead guilty; that decision is always the client’s. Our job is to fully inform our client about his/her options and support them completely in their decision, including taking cases to trial when that is the client's decision. Often times people choose to accept misdemeanor pleas when they are facing felonies.
While we strongly disagree with the characterizations made in this review, we are bound by legal and ethical obligations that prevent us from discussing case details publicly. That said, we are confident in the work that was done and stand by our firm’s values of professionalism, client advocacy, and integrity.
We wish this individual the best moving forward, Albarran & Joyce

reviews.text: Hannah and Tripp were incredible to work with. They guided us through every step of creating our operating agreement, forming our LLC, and finalizing our lease agreement. Their responsiveness, clarity, and willingness to hop on calls or coordinate directly with our leasing agent made the experience seamless. We couldn’t have asked for a better team, highly recommend!

My wife and I live on the west coast and have family property in Arkansas. We needed legal representation to negotiate an easement with the neighbors who are selling their property. It's very challenging to find an attorney when all you have to rely on are reviews and your intuition and experience to select the right attorney. We could not have been happier (or more fortunate) to work with Charles (Tripp) Halbert and his team.

Being across country is challenging enough, but the peace of mind that Tripp gave us throughout the entire process was the best I've ever had working with any attorney. What I appreciated most was his approach to fair and simple pricing. He offered us a flat rate for the services he provided with the understanding that if things changed and required additional fees, he'd let us know up front so there were no surprises. That's rare, and very much appreciated.

I really enjoyed working with Tripp. He's dependable, professional, and reliable. I've worked with a few attorneys in my life and can honestly say with no hesitation or any reservations that you will be well served working with Tripp. Highly recommend!

reviews.text: If you’re looking for a lawyer that isn’t going to be just your attorney but also a part of your family/friend, look no further. I’ve known Alfred for years and I met him through my career. Never in a million years did I expect or anticipate ever personally needing his services, until a family member of mine found himself battling addiction. My family member through substance charges and and substance abuse charges alone was facing 126 years. He spent roughly 18 months in rehab at his own decision and was being offered 30yrs with 10 suspended, serve time on 20 at 90% while being represented by the public defender. When the public defender laughed at him and told him that would be the best offer he would ever get. He hired a different private lawyer and that lawyer never even communicated with the prosecutor about him and was going to counter offer to a plea of 20, suspend 5, serve 15 at 90%. I personally caught that lawyer lying and we fired him and hired Alfred. Alfred immediately contacted the prosecutor and updated her about my family member, she had no idea that he had been in treatment or anything, she thought he was still in jail. Alfred pushed the case out as far as he could to give my family member a fighting chance in treatment and continued to communicate with the prosecutor. Today, he signed on 20, 14 suspended with 6 to serve in RPF aka CCP. Again, he was facing 126 years had he gone to trial. Alfred is a fighter and we will forever be grateful for him. While my family member is serving sometime, it could have been much much worse, so either way this is a win and it wouldn’t have happened without Alfred. You won’t regret hiring Mr. Alfred and he will definitely go above and beyond for you when you’re putting the work in to right your wrongs and turn a new leaf. You won’t regret hiring him. 🖤

reviews.text:
Can I give this place a 0 star?  I had to have Dana Watson as an Attorney Ad Litem (attorney for my children), after it was ordered by the judge in my case.  She did a terrible job, and my children agree.  She had her mind made up before the case started after talking to my children once and making up her mind in one month; violated my constitutional rights in the beginning of 2022 by not allowing me access to a key witness in the case; slandered me in the firm's office in June 2024 by stating that I avoided service when I obviously didn't; and, my children now refer to her as names I can't type here.

She also refused to serve me in person and decided that an email would suffice, when a 1st year legal student knows that method isn't constitutional.

The firm also decided to charge me $175 for service fees, when I was never served in the case.

These are all facts, and I can prove them.

DO NOT use this firm for any legal services.  I wasn't granted a choice.  You all have a choice.
Jamie Bingham is a great lawyer. He cares and  he knows his law .   He takes care of his clients.
I like Springdale in General although the curry is growing fast I love the ethnic blend of culture and community here

EDIT: Elizabeth called me and sincerely apologized. While I still have grounds to move on with a different firm, I appreciate her actions. It takes guts to do what she did, and she earned a lot of my respect back. I believe she is a good person.

ORIGINAL REVIEW:

My interactions with Tolley & Brooks Law Firm began on 9/25/25 after a referral from Nyman Turkish, who suggested them due to proximity and their ability to handle my case more intimately.

First contacts:
9/25/2025
10/03/2025
10/10/2025
10/15/2025
10/16/2025 (By this time, preliminary paperwork was completed and mailed back in. Attorney Elizabeth Rose called, and we secured her access to SSA.gov so she could file on my behalf.)

During the first few weeks, communication focused on consent forms and basic paperwork. I completed and returned everything immediately and provided SSA.gov access because I believed Elizabeth’s experience would help ensure the application was worded properly and, in turn, improve my chances of approval.

After two weeks of waiting, I followed up on 10/31/2025 and was told by Marilyn, “Let me touch base with Elizabeth and I’ll call you back.” No call back.

I called again on 11/04/2025 and 11/07/2025 — same response, no call back. As you'll see below, this became a pattern and was essentially the extent of my communication with Tolley & Brooks over five months.

On 11/07/2025, Marilyn mentioned there “may have been a miscommunication” about my phone number. I’ve only ever had one number, and they had previously used it successfully on 10/10/2025, so.. Your guess is as good as mine.

11/13/2025 — I called again. No call back.
12/01/2025 — I called and was told I’d receive a call back. None came.
12/03/2025 — They called, but we did not connect. I must've not noticed the missed call because the next contact was on 12/30/2025 when I reached out again.

01/05/2026 — I called, received a call back six hours later but missed it. I returned the call three hours after that and briefly spoke with someone for 3 minutes. I'm unsure what transpired on this call.

01/30/2026 — I called and was told my application had been submitted on 10/16/2025 and we were simply waiting on SS's decision.

02/03/2026 — I called to confirm whether it was submitted on 10/16 or 12/16 because I thought I misheard. This is important information because, typically, these applications take anywhere from 3-6 months to receive a decision. Knowing precisely when mine was submitted would give me a better idea of when I should be expecting a decision. Marilyn again affirmed “10/16/2025.”

02/04/2026 & 02/16/2026— Out of curiosity, I logged into SSA.gov and discovered no application had been submitted. This was OVER FOUR MONTHS after my first contact. The 10/16/2025 date repeatedly given to me was when an application was started — not completed. I called and shared this information. Marilyn seemed shocked and again said she would contact Elizabeth and call me back. No call back.

02/20/2026 — I called again. Same response. No call back.

02/25/2026 — After speaking with my local SS office, who confirmed there was no recent disability application (only my denied 2023 filing), I informed Marilyn I would be taking my business elsewhere in a polite yet stern manner.

TL;DR: Communication was extremely poor — probably the worst I’ve experienced with any business. Reception repeatedly assured me things were being handled, yet no application was ever submitted. I spoke with my lawyer only once in those five months, during our 29-minute call to verify SSA.gov access. I returned paperwork immediately, remained cooperative and polite, and missed only two calls (returning one near-immediately). There appears to be a serious internal disconnect, and I do not feel I received the "they care about their clients" treatment you see in other Google reviews here. Do I think they're bad people? No, not at all. Do I think my case should've been handled better? Absolutely. Maybe I'm a statistical anomaly, but this was my experience, and I hope others have a better one.

Kindly,
Ryan

My NIGHTMARE with Social Security Administration is OVER.  Thank God.  The SSA told me in February 2016 that I had been overpaid $57,300.00 and to PAY IT BACK.
Imagine my horror.
The SSA stopped sending the money in question to my account and insisted I pay back the $57,300.00.  The NOT KNOWING my fate from February 2016 to October 2016 was extremely distressing.  It was due to a clerical error by a clerk when I applied for SSA benefit.
My efficient and diligent Lawyer, Elizabeth BT Rose, went to the SSA website Program Operation Manual and found a document (GN04003.100) that stated "if more than 4 years after the date of the notice of the social determination, we discover the error, we cannot correct the error since the end result of revising the record would be unfavorable to the claimant (GN04010.020)".  It had been 8+ years.
I have been REINSTATED!  THANK my compassionate and caring Lawyer, Elizabeth BT Rose of the Tolley and Brooks Law Firm, P.A., Fayeteville, AR.
Expensive and terribly disappointing.
